description: The site is rebuilt step-by-step into a unified Design System 2025, using strict phase gates and safe modifications only.

phases.name: Preflight Integrity Scan
tasks: Scan the entire project structure and list all HTML, CSS, JS, assets, and localization files.
Validate all HTML files for unclosed tags, invalid nesting, duplicate IDs, and broken ARIA roles.
Check all CSS files for parse errors, unmatched braces, deprecated selectors, orphaned utilities, or invalid @media rules.
Verify that JS files load without console errors and that all script tags reference valid paths.
Confirm all translation dictionaries load correctly and contain no malformed keys or encoding issues.
Generate a preflight integrity report summarizing all findings before Phase 1 begins.

phases.name: Token Extraction
tasks: Extract all colors, spacing, typography, radius, shadows, and z-index values from CSS files.
Create a structured token list with id, raw value, usage notes, and file/line reference.
Detect and annotate duplicated or near-duplicate values.
Write the final token inventory in Markdown.
Do not modify any CSS in Phase 1.

phases.name: Token Unification
tasks: Group tokens by semantic meaning (brand, surface, border, text, spacing, radius, shadow, breakpoint).
Propose a normalized token map.
Map all legacy values to new canonical tokens.
Produce a migration table.
No CSS or HTML is modified in Phase 2.

phases.name: Card System Rebuild
tasks: Identify all card-like components.
Define a single base `.card` component.
Extend semantic variants from the base using tokens.
Remove CSS duplication between card implementations.
Only update card-related CSS blocks and classes.

phases.name: Navigation Rebuild
tasks: Identify all navigation implementations.
Define a single, reusable navigation component.
Refactor legacy or duplicate nav styles and markup.
Ensure JavaScript hooks remain stable or are upgraded.
Do not modify non-navigation components.

phases.name: Hero Rebuild
tasks: Catalog all hero sections.
Identify shared patterns.
Define a unified `.hero` system with clear variants.
Refactor hero markup to use the unified structure.
Do not touch non-hero sections.

phases.name: Layout Unification
tasks: Map all distinct layout systems.
Define canonical layout utilities.
Replace ad-hoc margin/padding hacks with spacing tokens.
Use canonical layouts for timelines, feature sections, and card grids.
Do not modify typography or icon rules.

phases.name: Typography System
tasks: Identify all heading levels, body text, labels, badges, and captions.
Extract font-sizes, line-heights, weights, and letter-spacing.
Define a single typography scale.
Map existing elements to the new system using tokens.
Remove redundant or conflicting font rules.

phases.name: Iconography System
tasks: Inventory all icons and their sizes, colors, and wrappers.
Define a canonical icon component.
Use canonical classes and tokens for icons.
Remove inconsistent inline styles.
Do not change text content or layout behavior.

phases.name: Component Harmonization
tasks: Scan for overlapping or legacy component variants.
Reduce each component family to a minimal set of reusable, token-driven components.
Document and remove deprecated variants after replacements are wired.
Enforce cross-page consistency.

phases.name: Full HTML Cleanup
tasks: Remove obsolete classes, duplicate wrappers, and unused structural containers following the DS2025 cleanup rules.
Normalize all section structures to the DS2025 canonical pattern (section + container) while keeping zero visual regression.
Preserve i18n bindings, data-i18n-allow-html, ARIA attributes, semantic tags, and heading hierarchy.
Ensure each page remains fully functional, accessible, and aligned with the DS2025 utilities (component-card, component-grid, component-banner, component-form, btn tokens).
Validate HTML integrity: no unclosed tags, duplicate IDs, nested anchors, or broken lists.
Confirm no hard-coded text nodes remain after cleanup (i18n enforced everywhere).
Confirm cross-page uniformity between surface / alt / cta sections according to the DS2025 alternation system.

phases.name: Global QA
tasks: Execute a full-system QA sweep across all public pages using DS2025 QA rules, ensuring zero visual regression.
Detect and correct layout inconsistencies: misaligned containers, uneven section spacing, incorrect surface/alt transitions, and broken responsive behavior.
Audit all interactive components: hover states, focus states, button behaviors, navigation dropdowns, and collapsible items.
Validate semantic HTML and accessibility: ARIA attributes, heading hierarchy, landmark roles, label connections, and keyboard navigability.
Confirm every translation key (NL/EN) renders correctly with no fallback, no garbled characters, and no missing bindings.
Test internal and external links, buttons, CTAs, forms, and navigation flows for correctness and stability.
Review cross-browser behavior (Chrome, Firefox, Safari/Edge baseline) to ensure consistent layouts and interactions.
Re-check integration between DS2025 utilities (component-card, component-grid, section modifiers, btn tokens) across all pages.
Guarantee that all findings are corrected inline before closing Phase 11, preserving functionality and accessibility across the entire site.

phases.name: Stability Verification
tasks: Run a full-system stability and regression sweep across all pages, components, utilities, and flows.
Verify that no JavaScript console errors, warnings, or runtime exceptions occur during navigation, interactions, or dynamic sections.
Ensure all CSS files parse cleanly with zero syntax errors, unmatched braces, unknown selectors, or deprecated utilities.
Validate that all critical flows operate correctly: navigation, forms, CTAs, language switching (i18n), course-details sections, countdown timers, and intake processes.

Confirm that the DS2025 design system remains fully intact: tokens render correctly, component utilities behave consistently, section modifiers alternate correctly, and layout utilities preserve visual integrity.
Re-audit translation bindings to guarantee that no fallback strings, missing keys, or encoding issues remain after Phase 11.
Document the final state of the system: token definitions, component families, section patterns, layout utilities, and the DS2025 structural rules.
Produce a final stability confirmation message: 'Phase 12 Completed. Awaiting next phase.'

phases.name: Full CSS Cleanup & Rationalization
tasks: Scan all CSS files (main.css, base.css, utilities, component blocks) to identify unused selectors, duplicate rules, dead utilities, and legacy overrides.
Map each selector to actual HTML usage across all public pages to ensure nothing in use is removed.
Remove Deprecated CSS: eliminate selectors retired in earlier phases (e.g., legacy hero/card/layout utilities, old navigation rules, section modifiers no longer referenced).
Consolidate duplicate or near-duplicate rules into canonical DS2025 utilities using tokens only.
Normalize spacing, colors, borders, radii, shadows, and typography to match the final DS2025 token map.
Preserve functional selectors required for JavaScript hooks, accessibility states, ARIA controls, and RTL/i18n behaviors.
Validate CSS integrity: ensure no unmatched braces, parse errors, SCSS remnants, malformed @media blocks, or invalid nesting remain.
Minimize CSS footprint by removing comments, resetting old hacks, and compressing redundant declarations into shared utilities.
Guarantee that all components (hero, navbar, cards, grids, banners, forms, CTAs, icons) render identically before and after cleanup — zero visual regression.
Prepare a final CSS audit report summarizing removed selectors, merged utilities, token usage, and any weaknesses still identified before final confirmation.
Output final confirmation: 'Phase 13 Completed. CSS system fully rationalized.'

phases.name: Performance Optimization
tasks: Audit all network requests to ensure minimal blocking resources and optimal loading order.
Optimize critical rendering path using proper preload, preconnect, and fetchpriority hints.
Ensure all CSS and JS bundles are lightweight, modular, and loaded efficiently.
Eliminate unused JS modules, redundant event listeners, and legacy scripts.
Improve Core Web Vitals: reduce CLS shifts, optimize LCP elements, and ensure fast FID responsiveness.
Review caching behavior and ensure proper cache-control headers are reflected in project code (where applicable).
Validate lazy-loading for non-critical media and non-essential background assets (without compressing images).
Ensure third-party integrations (fonts, icons, embeds) load efficiently with fallback strategies.
Generate a performance report summarizing improvements before closing Phase 14.
Output final confirmation: 'Phase 14 Completed. Performance optimization finished.'

phases.name: Responsive Layout & Spacing Harmonization
tasks: Audit all pages across mobile, phablet, and tablet breakpoints to detect any misalignment, overflow, or broken layout elements.
Center all sections uniformly, ensuring consistent horizontal alignment across all pages and devices.
Normalize vertical spacing between every section using DS2025 spacing tokens to achieve a clean, modern rhythm.
Ensure equal padding left/right on all sections to prevent left-shift or right-shift issues.
Fix responsive grids so cards, CTAs, icons, forms, and banners collapse cleanly and maintain readability on small screens.
Remove redundant margin/padding helpers and consolidate spacing into unified DS2025 utilities.
Guarantee consistent section transitions (surface → alt → cta) with perfect spacing alignment on all breakpoints.
Ensure all text blocks, icons, and images scale proportionally and remain centered on mobile.
Harmonize card spacing, grid gaps, CTA padding, and container widths to match the modern visual identity.
Validate responsive state for navigation, hero sections, forms, timelines, benefit grids, and partner showcases.
Confirm zero overflow, zero clipped content, zero horizontal scroll, and zero spacing anomalies.
Output final confirmation: 'Phase 15 Completed. Responsive & spacing harmonization finished.'

phases.name: SEO Optimization — On-Page & Technical
tasks: Optimize all HTML pages following Google Search Essentials and modern SEO guidelines.
Rewrite <title> and <meta description> for every page with precise, professional NL/EN versions.
Ensure correct canonical URLs, remove duplicates, and standardize meta structure.
Generate official course meta descriptions (NL + EN) in INTEC tone for all opleiding pages.
Implement complete OpenGraph metadata: og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, and Twitter Card equivalents.
Verify one-and-only-one <h1> per page and rebuild heading hierarchy (H1 → H2 → H3) with no violations.
Create a 'Heading Hierarchy Map' for each page to detect misplaced or empty headings.
Fix all pages with H1 in footers, orphaned H3s, or unordered heading levels.
Normalize semantic HTML to align accessibility + SEO structure with DS2025 rules.
Validate final SEO output through Lighthouse, semantic validation, and HTML verification.
Guarantee zero visual regression during metadata and heading cleanup.
Output: 'Phase 16 Completed. SEO optimization finished.'

phases.name: Content Quality & Semantic Enhancement
tasks: Perform a full content audit across NL and EN versions of all pages to ensure clarity, correctness, and consistency.
Rewrite weak or duplicated sentences, ensuring academic/professional INTEC tone across the entire site.
Strengthen semantic markers: lists, definition blocks, callouts, emphasis tags, and structured paragraphs.
Add accessible labels, aria descriptions, and semantic hints where reading flow benefits.
Ensure course pages include consistent learning outcomes, duration descriptions, and call-to-action phrasing.
Normalize terminology: unify wording for registration steps, program modules, dates, instructor references, and intake instructions.
Check for filler text, placeholder content, or repeated phrases and replace with meaningful academic descriptions.
Review microcopy (buttons, form labels, hints, placeholders) to improve clarity and reduce friction.
Enhance the information architecture by ensuring section intros, subheadings, and body paragraphs follow a coherent narrative.
Revalidate i18n bindings so improved content exists cleanly in both NL and EN dictionaries.
Guarantee zero layout impact while improving semantic clarity and readability.
Output: 'Phase 17 Completed. Content quality and semantics enhanced.'

phases.name: i18n SEO, Schema.org & Dynamic Sitemap
tasks: Improve i18n SEO for NL and EN, ensuring all SEO strings (titles, descriptions, OG/Twitter fields) have accurate translations.
Validate translation semantics and enable data-i18n-allow-html on all SEO-bound elements that require HTML-safe rendering.
Perform a linguistic accuracy pass for NL and EN to ensure professional, official tone for course/program pages.
Implement Schema.org JSON-LD blocks for Course, EducationalOrganization, and BreadcrumbList across all opleiding pages.
Populate Course schema with duration, price (if available), level, training location, accreditation (VDAB), and next start date.
Add global EducationalOrganization schema referencing INTEC Brussel including logo, address, sameAs links, and contact metadata.
Add BreadcrumbList schema for hierarchical navigation on all major pages (home → opleidingen → specific opleiding).
Generate a full sitemap.xml including all public pages: home, opleiding pages, inschrijven, contact, digipunt, opleidingen overview, team, vacancies, cvdb login, and overons.
Ensure sitemap URLs follow canonical structure and include lastmod, changefreq, and priority metadata.
Validate that multilingual pages (NL/EN) include <loc> and <xhtml:link rel="alternate" hreflang=""> entries in sitemap.
Run a full SEO structural validation after implementing schema and sitemap to ensure Google Rich Results compatibility.
Output final confirmation: 'Phase 18 Completed. i18n SEO, Schema.org, and dynamic sitemap finished.'

phases.name: Backend Integration (Spring Boot + JPA + REST API)
tasks: Set up a new Spring Boot backend project using Maven with the packages: controller, service, repository, model, config, dto.
Configure MySQL/PostgreSQL connection and create a persistent database for all dynamic website content.
Implement a TranslationService that replaces en.js and nl.js by loading all i18n keys from the database.
Create REST endpoints for translations: GET /api/translations, POST /api/translations, PUT /api/translations/{id}.
Migrate all translation keys from en.js and nl.js into the database (automatic import script).
Implement a Content Management API for: About Page, Team Info, Courses Data, Factors, Digipunt, and Homepage Sections.
Create an Admin Authentication system using Spring Security + JWT.
Build Admin endpoints for CRUD operations: /api/admin/courses, /api/admin/sections, /api/admin/team, /api/admin/factors.
Ensure all data returned by the API is multilingual (NL/EN) using i18n columns per entity.
Connect the frontend to the backend: replace static content with live API data while keeping DS2025 structure intact.
Implement caching layers for translations and static sections to improve performance.
Create dynamic sitemap generation via a backend endpoint: GET /sitemap.xml.
Ensure canonical URLs, SEO metadata, structured data (JSON-LD), and OpenGraph tags are populated dynamically from the API.
Verify that all DS2025 utilities remain untouched and no frontend regression occurs.
Produce the final backend documentation: models, endpoints, JSON schemas, authentication flow, and integration manual.
Output final confirmation message: 'Phase 19 Completed. Backend integration finished.'
